title: fsl/fslf/fss/fssf tags + from() family
manager: The file/format tags — fsl, fslf, fss, fssf — distinguishing a bare machine from a factory, a system, and a system-factory, plus a `from()` family for constructing from each. This is the type-tagging that lets tooling and the loader know what shape of thing a document holds, and `from()` is the one uniform entry point for instantiating any of them rather than four bespoke constructors.
cs: fsl/fslf/fss/fssf tags (machine/factory/system/system-factory); `from()` construction family; `sm` deprecated synonym; the document-kind taxonomy.
